sections.paragraph:
fut. יִגֹּף.-

(1) to smite, especially used of Jehovah striking men with some plague, Ex. 7:27 especially with a fatal plague, with sickness, or death, 1Sa 25:38 Psa 89:24 2Ch 21:18. In another sense God is said to smite a people before their enemies, i.e. to permit them to be smitten by their enemies, 1Sa 4:3, “why hath Jehovah smitten us to-day before the Philistines?” Jud. 20:35 2Ch 13:15 21:14 compare Niphal.

(2) to push, e.g. as horned cattle, Exo 21:35 as man against man, Exo 21:22.

(3) to strike against (with the foot), Pro 3:23 Psa 91:12.

Niphal נִגַּף to be smitten, defeated (as an army), Jud. 20:36 1Sa 4:10 commonly followed by לִפֳנֵי to be smitten (and flee) before an enemy, Lev 26:17 Deu 28:7 Jud. 20:36 1Sa 4:2 2Sa 8:17, etc.

Hithpael, i.q. Kal No. 3, to strike against (used of the foot), Jer 13:16.

Hence מַגֵּפָה, and-
